data.title: Sumitomo Mitsui Trust's Nine-Month Net Income Up 18%
Panama court voids CK Hutchison port contracts, clouding sales plan
data.description:
STORY: Future operations of the Panama Canal face fresh doubt after the country's Supreme Court annulled key port contracts held by a subsidiary of Hong Kong-based CK Hutchison.

Thursday's decision leaves the future ownership of some Panama Canal facilities unclear.

The country's highest court said that it found the laws and acts underpinning the concession contract between the state and Panama Ports Company unconstitutional.

Panama Ports Company, a CK Hutchison subsidiary, operates container terminals at the canal's Pacific and Atlantic entrances, separate from the waterway's operations.

It has held the contracts since the 1990s.

The decision could disrupt CK Hutchison's proposed $23 billion sale of dozens of ports worldwide, including the Panamanian terminals, to a consortium led by BlackRock and Mediterranean Shipping Company.

The three companies did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

CK Hutchison's Hong Kong listed shares dropped around 5% Friday following the news. 

The ruling comes amid a growing U.S.-China rivalry over global trade routes and is seen as a win for Washington.

President Donald Trump has pushed to curb Chinese influence over the Panama Canal, which carries about 5% of global maritime trade.

data.title: Raytron's Automotive Thermal Camera Integrated into over 20 Vehicle Models
data.description:
Raytron announced that it has established partnerships with over 15 leading OEMs, and its automotive thermal cameras have been deployed across more than 20 vehicle models, marking a major milestone in night vision and all-weather perception for intelligent vehicles. Animal and pedestrian collisions remain a critical pain point in nighttime road safety. While conventional lights typically provide a visibility range of 100 to 150 meters, Raytron's automotive thermal cameras are more than double that reach, extending detection to over 300 meters.

By capturing high-contrast thermal signatures, the system identifies pedestrians, animals, and obstacles in total darkness far earlier than visible-light cameras. When integrated into a thermal-based AEB system, as deployed on the Zeekr 9X, the vehicle can initiate autonomous emergency braking when drivers fail to perceive hazards at night, significantly reducing the risk of nighttime collisions. Commercial vehicles, such as mining trucks and heavy-duty freight haulers, frequently operate in dust, fog, sand storms, and low-visibility environments.

These environmental "obscurants" significantly degrade the performance of visible-light cameras and LiDAR sensors. In contrast, Long-Wave Infrared (LWIR) technology, operating in the 8-14mm wavelength range, offers superior atmospheric penetration. For heavy-duty vehicles with extended braking distances, early obstacle detection is a safety necessity rather than merely a feature.

Raytron's automotive thermal camera are already deployed across multiple commercial vehicles, such as Breton,argobot, and Zhizi Automobile, supporting safer operations in mining, logistics, and industrial transportation. In L4 autonomous driving, a single sensor may struggle to address complex corner cases. Sensor Fusion--combining visible-light camera, LiDAR, 4D mmWave imaging radar, and infrared thermal imaging-- has become the industry standard for robust perception.

Thermal imaging provides a unique edge by excelling in nighttime and low-visibility scenarios and enhancing the detection of vulnerable road users (VRUs) through their thermal signatures. Raytron's automotive Thermal cameras have already been successfully integrated into the Robotaxi of DiDi Autonomous Driving, providing a critical perception layer for safe and reliable driving.

data.title: Ambuja Cements Q3 PAT 2.04 billion rupees
Surging Profits Propel HD Hyundai Stock Rally
data.description:
Global demand for smart power solutions is surging as utilities race to meet net-zero targets; the switchgear and transformer market is forecast to grow north of 6% annually through 2029, driven by electrification and grid modernization. HD Hyundai Electric is riding that tide, reinforcing its position as a key supplier for large-scale renewable integration and industrial electrification.

HD Hyundai's footprint mirrors the continent-level push for low-carbon infrastructure: newly awarded HVDC contracts in Latin America and the Middle East signal growing demand for Korean engineering prowess amid a worldwide utility investment boom predicted to surpass USD 1.0tn this decade. Strategic partnerships are reinforcing supply chains and keeping capital flowing through export credit agencies.

What sets HD Hyundai apart is its blend of vertical integration and digital innovation: the firm couples factory automation with AI-driven condition monitoring to cut commissioning times, while its modular substations meet fast-track deployment needs in emerging grids. Strategic alliances with European grid operators and targeted investments in green hydrogen infrastructure keep it ahead in a competitive global arena.

HD Hyundai's business plays across three macro segments: power systems (HVAC/HVDC switchgear and transformers), industrial power (motors, drives, plant automation) and smart-grid/digital solutions (asset monitoring, energy storage, EV traction). Its vertical integration lets it ship 765-kilovolt gear that few rivals can match, keeping margins healthy.

Profit surge

HD Hyundai's Q3 25 tale was one of accelerating momentum. Sales jumped 26.2% y/y to KRW 995.4bn, lifted by a widening base of overseas projects and a steady domestic slate. The power segment alone soared 87.7% y/y, driven by high-voltage transformer and GIS wins, highlighting how HD Hyundai Electric is capitalizing on global grid modernization spending.

Profitability followed suit, with operating profit widening 50.9% y/y to KRW 247.1bn. Driven by tighter procurement, scale efficiencies from recent plant expansions, and the higher-margin mix of transformer and modular switchgear sales, the firm managed to control input costs despite ongoing volatility in steel and semiconductor prices.

Net profit rose 63.9% to KRW 191.1bn, showing that the company's upgraded supply chain processes and stronger won-dollar spread were translating into cleaner earnings, even as it reinvests cash into automation lines and R&D labs. Momentum extended to the order book: surged 71.4% y/y to USD 1.2bn, while backlog climbed 29.3% to USD 7.0bn thanks to multi-year export contracts.

Shares rally

HD Hyundai’s breakout revenue surge has fueled a stock rally of 102.5% over past one year, lifting its market capitalization to KRW 32.4tn and pushing the valuation to a premium 35.1x projected 2026 earnings—well above its three-year average of 28.2x. Investors are clearly banking on the firm’s momentum in high-voltage systems and digital grid upgrades, even as the rich multiple raises the bar for execution.

The analyst community shares that upbeat view: the consensus target sits at KRW 961,124, implying about 6.7% upside from today’s levels, while the most optimistic call stretches to KRW 1.0m, signaling a 33.2% potential jump. 20 out of 21 analysts cover HD Hyundai with “Buy” ratings, a reflection of confidence in sustained order-book growth and margin expansion.

Risks ahead

In the electrification arena, HD Hyundai radiates as a trusted engineer—delivering high-voltage solutions, digital grid platforms, global partnerships, disciplined capex, and capturing resilient-grid demand with steady profitability momentum.

However, HD Hyundai now faces the challenge of staying ahead of raw-material swings and supply-chain bottlenecks for specialized transformers and switchgear. Currency volatility and long lead times for semiconductors and high-purity copper could pinch margins if not managed tightly, while rising competition keeps pricing discipline firm. The company’s resilience will depend on delivering modular, digital-ready assets quickly, maintaining disciplined capex, and turning its expanding backlog into consistent cash flow even as global electrification strategies evolve under geopolitical and fiscal uncertainty.

data.title: Euro zone consumers up 5-year inflation forecast to record high, ECB poll shows
data.description:
FRANKFURT, Jan 30 (Reuters) - Euro zone consumers raised their longer-term inflation expectations to a record high in December, a European Central Bank poll showed on Friday, implying prices were seen growing faster than the ECB's target pace for years to come.

ECB President Christine Lagarde and colleagues have been saying for months the central bank for the euro zone is "in a good place" after seeing inflation fall to its 2% target, and market expectations remain largely anchored around that level.

But the ECB's latest Consumer Expectations Survey showed households had nudged up their inflation expectations five years ahead to 2.4% in December, the highest reading since the survey began in 2022 and a 20-basis-point increase from November.

The median respondent saw inflation at 2.8% over the next 12 months, unchanged from November, and at 2.6% in three years' time, up from 2.5% a month earlier.

Still, ECB policymakers, who use these findings in their policy deliberations, could take comfort in the fact that consumers still expected inflation to come down over the poll's time horizon.

The ECB is expected to hold its policy rate unchanged at 2% for the foreseeable future, but uncertainty relating, among other factors, to U.S. President Donald Trump's unpredictable economic policy has kept investors and policymakers on tenterhooks.

(Reporting by Francesco CanepaEditing by Peter Graff)

U.S. Futures Fall, European Stocks Mostly Higher
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were down 0.8% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.7%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 climbed 0.2% in morning trading. Alten added 12.1% and Swatch Group rose 6.9%. On the other hand, Signify sunk 9.6%, and SKF Series B dropped 6%. The FTSE 100 was flat. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 0.2% and Germany's DAX climbed 0.5%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index climbed 0.3% to 94.19.

In commodities, Brent crude dropped 1.8% to $68.32 a barrel, and WTI crude fell 1.8% to $64.22 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 0.9% to 38.96 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield was flat at 2.842%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury rose by 3 basis points to 4.271% from 4.24%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were down as Japan's Nikkei 225 index declined 0.1%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng fell 2.1%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite declined 1%.


-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.


Write to Barcelona editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-30-26 0351ET

Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, Ltd. Revises Non-Consolidated Earnings Guidance for the Fiscal Year Ending March 31, 2026
data.description:
Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, Ltd. revised non-consolidated earnings guidance for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. For the period, the company expects revenue to be JPY 890,000 million against JPY 810,000 million expected previously, Operating profit to be JPY 59,000 million against JPY 43,000 million expected previously, net income to be JPY 223,000 million against JPY 212,000 million expected previously. For the fiscal year ending March 2026, the company have updated its performance forecasts, considering factors such as the continued steady cargo movements in its vehicle transport business and the impact of the suspension of additional port entry fees by the United States Trade Representative (USTR).

In addition, by revising the assumptions for our full-year non-consolidated performance to reflect market improvements in the crude oil tanker business, we have upwardly revised the non-consolidated performance forecasts for each period.

data.title: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, Ltd. Announces Retirement of Toshinobu Shinoda as Representative Director, Executive Vice President and Executive Officer, Effective April 1, 2026
data.description: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, Ltd. announced a change to its Representative Director according to resolutions made at the Board of Directors' meeting held January 30,2026. Reason of the change: Due to change of management; Toshinobu Shinoda, Current Representative Director, Executive Vice President and Executive Officer announced retirement effective April 1, 2026. Toshinobu Shinoda will be a non-representative director on April 1, 2026.

AstraZeneca Strengthens Its Weight Management Portfolio Through New Strategic Collaboration Agreement with CSPC Pharmaceuticals
data.description:
AstraZeneca strengthens its weight management portfolio through a new strategic collaboration agreement with CSPC Pharmaceuticals to advance the development of multiple next-generation therapies for obesity and type 2 diabetes across eight programmes. Under this agreement, the companies will initially progress four programmes, which utilise CSPC's advanced AI-driven peptide drug discovery platform and their proprietary LiquidGel once-monthly dosing platform technology. AstraZeneca will receive exclusive global rights outside of China to CSPC's once-monthly injectable weight management portfolio, comprising one clinical-ready asset, SYH2082, a long-acting GLP1R/GIPR agonist progressing into Phase I and three preclinical programmes with differing mechanisms designed to provide extended therapeutic benefits for people living with obesity and weight-related conditions.

AstraZeneca has optionality to pursue future metabolic programmes leveraging CSPC's proprietary LiquidGel once- monthly dosing platform technology and holds rights to deploy this across internal development programmes, broadening the potential applications of their sustained-release formulation. This could help improve treatment adherence and support strong patient preference for once-monthly regimens. This collaboration complements AstraZeneca's existing weight management portfolio which includes a growing pipeline of next generation treatments to address the complexities of obesity, and weight-related complications, pursuing multiple modalities and pathways in order to tailor treatment based on individual needs.

This includes elecoglipron (formerly AZD5004), a small molecule oral GLP1RA; AZD6234 a weekly injectable selective amylin receptor agonist (SARA); and AZD9550 a weekly injectable dual GLP-1/glucagon receptor agonist, as well as a number of preclinical assets. Financial Considerations. For access to eight programmes, as well as the advanced AI molecular design capabilities and their proprietary LiquidGel Once-monthly dosing platform Technology, CSPC will receive an upfront payment of $1.2 billion from AstraZeneca.

CSPC is also eligible to receive development and regulatory milestones of up to $3.5 billion across all programmes. CSPC will also be eligible for further commercialisation and sales milestones plus tiered royalties. The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory clearances.

Under the agreement, CSPC will progress ongoing development of the four programmes through Phase I completion, alongside the four new programmes. Following successful completion of Phase I, AstraZeneca will be responsible for further development and commercialisation in all territories outside of China. CSPC retains all rights for China, Taiwan, Hong Kong and Macau, and following successful approval, AstraZeneca has the right to opt-in to co-commercialise these products.

This agreement builds on existing strategic collaborations between AstraZeneca and CSPC.

Nestle India Q3 profit 10.18 billion rupees
Nestle India posts higher profit as tax cuts boost demand
data.description:
Jan 30 (Reuters) - Nestle India posted a 46% increase in third-quarter profit on Friday, aided by improving demand for packaged food items, including Maggi noodles and Nescafe coffee, after consumption tax cuts lifted spending.

The Indian unit of Swiss food major Nestle said net profit rose to 10.18 billion rupees ($110.77 million) for the quarter ended December 31, from 6.96 billion rupees a year earlier.

($1 = 91.9000 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Komal Salecha in Bengaluru; Editing by Sonia Cheema)

data.title: Wanhua Chemical to inject 19.1 billion yuan into unit
Adani group firm to swap part of dollar debt with rupee after top rating, sources say
data.description:
MUMBAI, Jan 30 (Reuters) - A unit of Indian billionaire Gautam Adani-led group plans to refinance part of its dollar debt with rupee funding over the next three months, following an upgrade of its credit rating to the highest level, two sources familiar with the matter said.

The unit, Adani Electricity Mumbai, has dollar bonds worth $831 million and $255 million maturing in February 2030 and July 2031, respectively, and plans to use the local borrowings to prepay part of this debt, the sources said on Friday.

The sources requested anonymity since they are not authorised to speak to media. The company did not respond to a Reuters email seeking comment.

Adani Electricity Mumbai's issuer as well as debt rating was raised by one notch by India Ratings earlier this week, making it India's first private power distribution company to achieve the highest credit rating locally, according to bankers.

The rating for 10 billion rupees ($108.8 million) of local debentures could be used to fund the prepayment of dollar bonds, the sources said.

"The upgrade allows the firm to replace existing dollar bonds with cheaper domestic funding, lowering its borrowing costs," one of the sources said.

"A local issue is expected to draw interest from long-only investors, including insurance companies and pension funds that typically invest only in top-rated securities," the person said.

In May 2025, another Adani group company, Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone, replaced dollar bonds with domestic notes, following a rating upgrade.

India's Adani group continues to face regulatory woes after the U.S. markets regulator earlier this month asked a U.S. court for help in serving summonses upon Gautam Adani and group executive Sagar Adani over alleged fraud and a $265 million bribery scheme.

Adani group has called the allegations "baseless" and said it would seek "all possible legal recourse" to defend itself.

While the group has tapped the dollar bond market, it has largely relied on the domestic market after shortseller Hindenburg Research in 2023 made allegations around its corporate governance practices. The group has denied the charges, and in September last year, India's markets regulator dismissed some of the allegations.

($1 = 91.9225 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Dharamraj Dhutia; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

By Dharamraj Dhutia

Swedbank Board Member Anna Mossberg Purchases Shares Worth 0.45 Million SEK
data.description:
On January 29, Anna Mossberg, a member of Swedbank's board of directors, purchased 1,320 shares in the bank. The shares were bought at a price of 351.70 SEK per share, amounting to a total transaction value of 464,200 SEK. The purchase was made on Nasdaq Stockholm. This information is disclosed in the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority's insider register.

Following this transaction, Mossberg now holds 4,173 shares in Swedbank, according to the ownership service Holdings.
